reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures all hypotheses: complete metric space (`[MetricSpace X] [CompleteSpace X]`), closed (`IsClosed`), nonempty (`Set.Nonempty`), bounded (`Bornology.IsBounded`, the standard Mathlib notion of bounded in a metric space), nested decreasing (`E n ⊇ E (n+1)`, matching Rudin's `⊃`), and diameter tending to 0 (`Tendsto (Metric.diam ∘ E) atTop (𝓝 0)`). The conclusion `∃ a, Set.iInter E = {a}` expresses exactly "the intersection consists of exactly one point". Indexing from 0 instead of 1 is inconsequential because of the monotonicity of the sets. All types and quantifier structures match the informal claim.
